title: Amazon ECS Task Definition
description: Schema for an Amazon Elastic Container Service (ECS) task definition. A task definition is required to run Docker containers in Amazon ECS and describes one or more containers that form your application, including container images, CPU and memory requirements, networking, volumes, and IAM roles.

family.description: The family name for the task definition. Up to 255 characters consisting of uppercase and lowercase letters, numbers, underscores, and hyphens.

taskDefinitionArn.description: The full Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the task definition.

revision.description: The revision of the task definition in a particular family. Each time a task definition is registered, the revision is incremented.

status.description: The status of the task definition.

containerDefinitions.description: A list of container definitions that describe the different containers that make up your task.

taskRoleArn.description: The short name or full Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM role that containers in this task can assume. All containers in this task are granted permissions that are specified in this role.

executionRoleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the task execution role that grants the Amazon ECS container agent permission to make AWS API calls on your behalf, including pulling container images and publishing container logs.

networkMode.description: The Docker networking mode to use for the containers in the task. For Fargate launch type, awsvpc mode is required.

volumes.description: A list of volume definitions that containers in your task can use.

requiresCompatibilities.description: The task launch type that Amazon ECS validates the task definition against.

cpu.description: The number of CPU units used by the task. Required for Fargate launch type. Valid values for Fargate: 256 (.25 vCPU), 512 (.5 vCPU), 1024 (1 vCPU), 2048 (2 vCPU), 4096 (4 vCPU), 8192 (8 vCPU), 16384 (16 vCPU).

memory.description: The amount of memory (in MiB) used by the task. Required for Fargate launch type.

pidMode.description: The process namespace to use for the containers in the task.

ipcMode.description: The IPC resource namespace to use for the containers in the task.

placementConstraints.description: An array of placement constraint objects to use for tasks. A maximum of 10 constraints are allowed per task.

inferenceAccelerators.description: The Elastic Inference accelerators to use for the containers in the task.

enableFaultInjection.description: Whether to enable fault injection for the task definition.

tags.description: The metadata that you apply to the task definition to help you categorize and organize them. Maximum of 50 tags per resource.

registeredAt.description: The Unix timestamp for when the task definition was registered.

deregisteredAt.description: The Unix timestamp for when the task definition was deregistered.

registeredBy.description: The principal that registered the task definition.

compatibilities.description: The launch type compatibilities, derived from the task definition requirements.

requiresAttributes.description: The container instance attributes required by your task.

ContainerDefinition.description: Container definitions describe the different containers that make up a task. Each task definition can contain multiple container definitions.

name.description: The name of a container. If you are linking multiple containers together in a task definition, the name of one container can be entered in the links of another container to connect the containers. Up to 255 characters.

image.description: The image used to start a container. This string is passed directly to the Docker daemon. Images in the Docker Hub registry are available by default. You can also specify other repositories with repository-url/image:tag or repository-url/image@digest.

cpu.description: The number of cpu units reserved for the container.

memory.description: The amount (in MiB) of memory to present to the container. This is the hard limit; if the container attempts to exceed this value, it is killed.

memoryReservation.description: The soft limit (in MiB) of memory to reserve for the container. When system memory is under contention, Docker attempts to keep the container memory to this soft limit.

links.description: Allows containers to communicate without the need for port mappings. This is only supported when the networkMode is bridge.

portMappings.description: The list of port mappings for the container. Port mappings allow containers to access ports on the host container instance.

essential.description: If the essential parameter of a container is marked as true and that container fails or stops for any reason, all other containers that are part of the task are stopped. Default is true.

entryPoint.description: The entry point that is passed to the container. This corresponds to the ENTRYPOINT in a Dockerfile.

command.description: The command that is passed to the container. This corresponds to the CMD instruction in a Dockerfile.

environment.description: The environment variables to pass to a container.

environmentFiles.description: A list of files containing the environment variables to pass to a container. File must be hosted in Amazon S3.

value.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Amazon S3 object containing the environment variable file.

mountPoints.description: The mount points for data volumes in your container.

volumesFrom.description: Data volumes to mount from another container.

sourceContainer.description: The name of another container within the same task definition to mount volumes from.

readOnly.description: If true, the container has read-only access to the volume.

secrets.description: The secrets to pass to the container. Secrets can be stored in AWS Secrets Manager or AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store.

name.description: The name of the secret. The value is set as an environment variable.

valueFrom.description: The secret to expose to the container. The full ARN of the Secrets Manager secret or the full ARN of the SSM parameter.

dependsOn.description: The dependencies defined for container startup and shutdown. A container can contain multiple dependencies.

condition.description: The dependency condition of the container.

startTimeout.description: Time duration (in seconds) to wait before giving up on resolving dependencies for a container.

stopTimeout.description: Time duration (in seconds) to wait before the container is forcefully killed if it does not exit normally on its own.

hostname.description: The hostname to use for your container.

user.description: The user to use inside the container. Can be specified as UID, UID:GID, user name, or user name:group name.

workingDirectory.description: The working directory to run commands inside the container in.

disableNetworking.description: When set to true, networking is off within the container.

privileged.description: When set to true, the container is given elevated privileges on the host container instance (similar to the root user).

readonlyRootFilesystem.description: When set to true, the container is given read-only access to its root file system.

dnsServers.description: A list of DNS servers that are presented to the container.

dnsSearchDomains.description: A list of DNS search domains that are presented to the container.

extraHosts.description: A list of hostnames and IP address mappings to append to the /etc/hosts file on the container.

dockerSecurityOptions.description: A list of strings to provide custom labels for SELinux and AppArmor multi-level security systems.

interactive.description: When true, you can deploy containerized applications that require stdin or a tty to be allocated.

pseudoTerminal.description: When true, a TTY is allocated.

dockerLabels.description: A key/value map of labels to add to the container.

ulimits.description: A list of ulimits to set in the container.

softLimit.description: The soft limit for the ulimit type.

hardLimit.description: The hard limit for the ulimit type.

systemControls.description: A list of namespaced kernel parameters to set in the container.

resourceRequirements.description: The type and amount of a resource to assign to a container, such as a GPU.

firelensConfiguration.description: The FireLens configuration for the container. Used to specify and configure a log router for container logs.

repositoryCredentials.description: The private repository authentication credentials to use.

credentialsParameter.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the secret containing the private repository credentials.

restartPolicy.description: The restart policy for the container.

enabled.description: Whether a restart policy is enabled for the container.

restartAttemptPeriod.description: A period of time (in seconds) that the container must run for before a restart can be attempted.

ignoredExitCodes.description: A list of exit codes that Amazon ECS ignores when deciding whether to restart a container.

credentialSpecs.description: A list of ARNs in SSM or Amazon S3 to a credential spec (CredSpec) file that configures the container for Active Directory authentication.

PortMapping.description: Port mappings allow containers to access ports on the host container instance to send or receive traffic.

containerPort.description: The port number on the container that is bound to the user-specified or automatically assigned host port.

hostPort.description: The port number on the container instance to reserve for your container.

protocol.description: The protocol used for the port mapping. Valid values are tcp and udp.

name.description: The name to use for the port mapping. This is used with Service Connect.

appProtocol.description: The application protocol that is used for the port mapping. This is used with Service Connect.

containerPortRange.description: The port number range on the container that is bound to the dynamically mapped host port range.

name.description: The name of the key-value pair.

value.description: The value of the key-value pair.

MountPoint.description: Details for a volume mount point that is used in a container definition.

sourceVolume.description: The name of the volume to mount. Must match the name of a volume in the task definition.

containerPath.description: The path on the container to mount the host volume at.

readOnly.description: If true, the container has read-only access to the volume.

LogConfiguration.description: The log configuration for the container. This parameter maps to LogConfig in the Docker container create command.

logDriver.description: The log driver to use for the container. For Fargate launch type, the supported log drivers are awslogs, splunk, and awsfirelens.

options.description: The configuration options to send to the log driver. For the awslogs driver the options are awslogs-group, awslogs-region, awslogs-stream-prefix, awslogs-create-group, and awslogs-datetime-format.

secretOptions.description: The secrets to pass to the log configuration.

HealthCheck.description: An object representing a container health check. The health check command and associated parameters defined for a container.

command.description: A string array representing the command that the container runs to determine if it is healthy. The string array must start with CMD to run the command arguments directly, or CMD-SHELL to run the command with the container's default shell.

interval.description: The time period in seconds between each health check execution. Default is 30 seconds.

timeout.description: The time period in seconds to wait for a health check to succeed before it is considered a failure. Default is 5 seconds.

retries.description: The number of times to retry a failed health check before the container is considered unhealthy. Default is 3.

startPeriod.description: The optional grace period in seconds to provide containers time to bootstrap before failed health checks count towards the maximum number of retries. Default is 0.

LinuxParameters.description: Linux-specific modifications that are applied to the container, such as capabilities, device mappings, and more.

capabilities.description: The Linux capabilities for the container that are added to or dropped from the default configuration provided by Docker.

add.description: The Linux capabilities for the container that have been added to the default configuration provided by Docker.

drop.description: The Linux capabilities for the container that have been removed from the default configuration provided by Docker.

devices.description: Any host devices to expose to the container.

hostPath.description: The path for the device on the host container instance.

containerPath.description: The path inside the container at which to expose the host device.

permissions.description: The explicit permissions to provide to the container for the device.

initProcessEnabled.description: Run an init process inside the container that forwards signals and reaps processes.

sharedMemorySize.description: The value for the size (in MiB) of the /dev/shm volume.

tmpfs.description: The container path, mount options, and size (in MiB) of the tmpfs mount.

size.description: The maximum size (in MiB) of the tmpfs volume.

maxSwap.description: The total amount of swap memory (in MiB) a container can use.

swappiness.description: Configures the container's memory swappiness behavior. A value of 0 causes swapping to not happen. A value of 100 causes pages to be swapped aggressively.

Volume.description: A data volume used in a task definition. For tasks that use the Fargate launch type, only efsVolumeConfiguration is supported.

name.description: The name of the volume. Up to 255 characters. This name is referenced in the sourceVolume parameter of container definition mountPoints.

host.description: Configuration for a bind mount host volume. Valid only when the scope is task.

sourcePath.description: The path on the host container instance to present to the container.

dockerVolumeConfiguration.description: Configuration for a Docker volume. Not supported for Fargate.

scope.description: The scope for the Docker volume. Determines its lifecycle.

autoprovision.description: If true, the Docker volume is created if it does not already exist.

driver.description: The Docker volume driver to use.

driverOpts.description: A map of Docker driver-specific options passed through.

labels.description: Custom metadata to add to your Docker volume.

efsVolumeConfiguration.description: Configuration for an Amazon Elastic File System volume.

fileSystemId.description: The Amazon EFS file system ID to use.

rootDirectory.description: The directory within the Amazon EFS file system to mount as the root directory inside the host.

transitEncryption.description: Whether to enable or disable encryption for Amazon EFS data in transit.

transitEncryptionPort.description: The port to use when sending encrypted data between the Amazon ECS host and the Amazon EFS server.

authorizationConfig.description: The authorization configuration details for the Amazon EFS file system.

accessPointId.description: The Amazon EFS access point ID to use.

iam.description: Whether to use the Amazon ECS task role for authorization.

fsxWindowsFileServerVolumeConfiguration.description: Configuration for an Amazon FSx for Windows File Server volume.

fileSystemId.description: The Amazon FSx for Windows File Server file system ID to use.

rootDirectory.description: The directory within the Amazon FSx for Windows File Server file system to mount as the root directory.

credentialsParameter.description: The authorization credential option to use.

domain.description: A fully qualified domain name hosted by an AWS Directory Service Managed Microsoft AD.

TaskDefinitionPlacementConstraint.description: An object representing a constraint on task placement in the task definition.

expression.description: A cluster query language expression to apply to the constraint.

ProxyConfiguration.description: The configuration details for the App Mesh proxy. For tasks that use the EC2 launch type, the container instances require at least version 1.26.0 of the container agent.

type.description: The proxy type. The only supported value is APPMESH.

containerName.description: The name of the container that will serve as the App Mesh proxy.

properties.description: The set of network configuration parameters to provide the Container Network Interface (CNI) plugin, specified as key-value pairs.

InferenceAccelerator.description: Details on an Elastic Inference accelerator.

deviceName.description: The Elastic Inference accelerator device name. The deviceName must also be referenced in a container definition as a ResourceRequirement.

deviceType.description: The Elastic Inference accelerator type to use.

EphemeralStorage.description: The amount of ephemeral storage to allocate for the task. This parameter is used to expand the total amount of ephemeral storage available, beyond the default amount, for tasks hosted on Fargate.

sizeInGiB.description: The total amount, in GiB, of ephemeral storage to set for the task. The minimum supported value is 21 GiB and the maximum supported value is 200 GiB.

RuntimePlatform.description: Information about the platform for the Amazon ECS service or task. For Fargate launch type, this is the operating system that the task definition targets.

Tag.description: The metadata that you apply to a resource to help you categorize and organize them.

key.description: One part of a key-value pair that make up a tag. A key is a general label that acts like a category for more specific tag values.

value.description: The optional part of a key-value pair that make up a tag. A value acts as a descriptor within a tag category (key).
